JOURNAL (current thesis state):
# Journal — Investing Agent

## 1. Market view

Universe broadly weak heading into the July earnings cluster; cash at 66.4% reflects patience rather than conviction. Seven positions across five sectors, all starter-to-moderate size. The next 40 days are the critical adjudication window: ATRLJ ~Jul 10, CORE-B ~Jul 22, VOLV/ESSITY ~Jul 24, RUSTA Q1 TBD, ASSA/THULE ~Jul 28–29. No urgency to add before those prints resolve. Rusta initiated this week as the cleanest new signal; ASSA-B deferred for a better entry post-Q2.

## 2. Holdings

**HM-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 4.9%
- **Thesis:** Karl-Johan Persson bought ~1.2 BSEK across six transactions in eight days into an 11% drawdown — one of the strongest insider signals possible on Swedish Large Cap. Starter position pending fundamental confirmation.
- **Sell if:** Profit warning, gross margin collapse, or confirmed structural market-share loss to Shein/Temu. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ESSITY-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Staples | 8.0%
- **Thesis:** Exceptional 90-day insider cluster — 512 MSEK, 7 buyers, 0 sellers — led by Industrivärden. Defensive staples compounder at ~14x P/E with 3.4% yield. Q2 ~Jul 24 is the next adjudicator.
- **Sell if:** Sustained margin deterioration where input costs structurally exceed pricing power, or Q2 shows revenue decline accelerating with margin compression.
- **Status:** `intact`

**THULE.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 3.5%
- **Thesis:** Ten insiders including chair, vice chair, CEO, and CFO bought ~14–25 MSEK across two waves. Quality mid-cap franchise 28% off highs; weakness is cyclical not structural. Q2 ~Jul 29 is the next adjudicator.
- **Sell if:** Insider turns net seller, operating margin compresses below ~13%, or Q2 reveals genuine earnings deterioration rather than cyclical softness.
- **Status:** `intact`

**VOLV-B.ST** — Core | Industrials | 4.8%
- **Thesis:** Industrivärden deployed ~290 MSEK in a single day near 52-week highs. Net cash ~58 BSEK, ~4% yield, 9.8% operating margin. Controlling owner buying near highs endorses forward earnings. Q2 ~Jul 24.
- **Sell if:** Industrivärden becomes net seller, profit warning, or Q2 reveals order intake collapse rather than cyclical normalisation.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ATRLJ-B.ST** — Core | Real Estate | 4.8%
- **Thesis:** Tagehus (controlling family) bought ~30 MSEK into Varma's forced 7%-discount block exit. Ericsson 58k sqm Hagastaden anchor lease provides fundamental support. Starter size given net debt/equity 101%. Q2 ~Jul 10.
- **Sell if:** Further forced-seller overhang without insider response; Q2 reveals deteriorating occupancy/lease renewals; or Ericsson lease proves less economically material than signalled.
- **Status:** `intact`

**CORE-B.ST** — Aggressive | Real Estate | 2.9%
- **Thesis:** Rutger Arnhult bought ~110 MSEK personally across two waves into a 50%-off, capitulating leveraged commercial real estate name. FCF positive (678 MSEK R12); this is a leverage/rate story, not a going-concern story. Q2 ~Jul 22.
- **Sell if:** Dilutive equity raise, covenant breach, LTV deterioration in Q2, or Arnhult becomes a net seller.
- **Status:** `intact`

**RUSTA.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 4.5%
- **Thesis:** Controlling owner Aforber Invest bought ~52 MSEK across 13 transactions (including a single 30.6 MSEK ticket) days after a positive Q4 print and dividend raise, into a -23% drawdown. Discount retail has structural counter-cyclical tailwind. Starter size pending verification of the D/E 243% leverage (likely IFRS-16-driven but unconfirmed).
- **Sell if:** Leverage confirmed as genuine financial debt rather than operating leases and the deleveraging path is unclear; same-store sales deteriorate; or Aforber Invest becomes a net seller.
- **Status:** `intact`

## 3. Watchlist

**ASSA-B.ST** — Broad insider cluster (CEO ~35 MSEK + 4 operating managers) across April–June. Q2 Jul 28 is the gate. Target entry ≤340 SEK; Latour block-sale overhang may deliver it. Revisit post-print or on pullback below 335.

**VIMIAN.ST** — Fidelio Vet + CEO bought ~128 MSEK over two weeks; signal is real but anchor-holder concentrated and FCF is -243 MEUR. Q2 ~Jul 29. Analyst would-buy-at 27 SEK; watch into the print, reassess fundamentals then.

**NOLA-B.ST** — Principal owner Herenco bought ~25 MSEK in three clustered June purchases; clean signal endorsing the CEO transition. Revenue declining multi-year (-3.2% YoY). Entry target ~47 SEK; prefer to wait for Q2 (Aug 5) print under new CEO before sizing.

**LIFCO-B.ST** — Strong insider cluster (3 distinct buyers, board member 2x, ~10 MSEK around Q1 print) on a quality compounder. Price at 307 is above the 275–285 entry zone. Wait for Q2 (~Jul 24) pullback or post-print confirmation; re-screen week of Jul 21.

**HEXA-B.ST** — 4 management buyers including CFO bought ~12.4 MSEK into a -20% drawdown; no sellers. Data quality flag: fundamentals block contains implausible values (P/E 0.9, FCF yield 154%) — cannot validate valuation. Q2 ~Jul 25. Starter defensible on insider signal alone if data is cleaned up; otherwise wait for print.

## 4. Lessons learned / open questions

- **Rusta fill vs limit:** PM set limit at 86 SEK, execution filled at 75.50 — meaningfully better entry. No lesson to change, but note that the market offered a 12% better price than the limit; the conservative limit was correct given elevated vol (48.6%).
- **Consumer Discretionary concentration:** HM + Thule + Rusta = ~13% of portfolio. No further Consumer Discretionary adds without rebalancing one of these, even if the insider signal is strong. The sector cap (25%) is not close, but three correlated names is enough.
- **Correlated real estate:** ATRLJ + CORE-B = ~7.7%, both leveraged Swedish commercial real estate. No further real estate adds without rebalancing one first.
- **Open question — earnings cluster:** ATRLJ (Jul 10), CORE-B (Jul 22), VOLV/ESSITY (Jul 24), ASSA/THULE (Jul 28–29) all land in a 19-day window. Consider whether any pre-print trimming is warranted, or whether current starter sizing provides sufficient cushion. Reassess at next weekly cycle once ATRLJ reports first.

## Daily decisions log

- 2026-06-25 sell 17 THULE.ST @ 203.60 SEK — Scout-flagged weakest holding, no catalyst; freeing Consumer Discretionary budget to fund STORY
- 2026-06-25 buy 205 STORY-B.ST @ 91.00 SEK — 45M SEK founder+CFO insider cluster into a post-downgrade dip; high-conviction Jul 29 catalyst, funded partly by Thule exit
- 2026-06-25 buy 55 SCST.ST @ 144.40 SEK — Broad insider cluster (9 buyers, 0 sellers, CEO + strategic owner) on a staples name; starter ahead of Q2


ANALYST NOTES on today's candidates:
--- Analyst note on HEXA-B.ST (Hexagon) ---
Note the fundamentals here are **garbled and unreliable**. P/E of 0.9, FCF yield of 157.7%, dividend yield of 21%, and EUR 55B revenue at EUR 30B FCF on a SEK 80 stock all signal a data error (likely currency/share-count mismatch). I cannot trust any of the Börsdata valuation figures as presented.

**Thesis**
The real signal is the insider cluster: 4 distinct buyers including the CFO, ~12.4M SEK net buy over 90 days, with the most recent purchases (Wenzel, ~4.4M SEK) landing as the stock sits 21% below its 200MA and 33% off its 52w high. Management buying meaningful size into a sustained drawdown is a credible reversal setup. Earnings ~Jul 25 is a near-term catalyst.

**Positives**
- Genuine insider cluster: 4 buyers, CFO included, no sellers — strongest possible Swedish signal.
- Recent buys are dated *into* the weakness (May–June), not stale.
- Deeply oversold technically (−21% vs 200MA, −33% vs 52wH) — room for mean reversion.
- Bolt-on M&A (ITRES) shows management still executing offensively.

**Concerns**
- Fundamentals data is clearly corrupted — I cannot validate valuation, margins, or true FCF from what I have.
- Revenue −5.9% YoY signals real demand softness; the drawdown isn't purely sentiment.
- vol30d 82.5% is extreme — this is a knife with wide swings, position must be sized for that.
- "CEO insider purchase" in news doesn't match the insider table (no CEO listed) — minor data inconsistency.

**Insider / news read**
Insider table is the cleanest input: unanimous buying, CFO + multiple management, recent and clustered. News corroborates (insider purchase moved the stock, plus an accretive acquisition). This is the strongest part of the case.

**Price read**
At 80.62 the stock is deeply oversold across every timeframe and well below where insiders are stepping in. Risk/reward favors a bounce, but high vol means entry timing matters.

**Verdict**
Strong insider signal into oversold weakness, but corrupted fundamentals prevent a high-conviction full-size bet — a starter sized for the volatility is right. BUY — starter.

  "ticker": "HEXA-B.ST",

  "verdict": "BUY — starter",
  "conviction": 3,
  "key_positive": "Unanimous insider cluster (4 buyers incl. CFO) buying into a 21%-below-200MA drawdown, a textbook Swedish reversal signal.",
  "key_concern": "Reported fundamentals are clearly corrupted (P/E 0.9, 157% FCF yield), so I cannot validate valuation or underlying business strength.",
  "would_buy_at": 81.0,
  "would_sell_at": 100.0,
  "notes_for_trader": "Insider signal is the whole thesis; ignore the garbled Börsdata valuation numbers. vol30d 82.5% — size small and expect swings. Earnings ~Jul 25 is the catalyst/risk. Sell trigger: insider buying stops or stock breaks below recent lows on volume."
}
```

--- Analyst note on RUSTA.ST (Rusta) ---
# Rusta (RUSTA.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Already-held discount retailer that sold off ~18% in a month on a "stable but slowing" Q4, while a single major shareholder (Aforber Invest AB) plowed 51.6M SEK in over a tight window — a strong vote of confidence near current levels. The setup is a value/insider-conviction rebound: solid FCF yield (9.7%), raised dividend, and an insider clearly buying the dip. Horizon is medium-term (weeks-to-months) for mean reversion back toward the 200ma, not a fast catalyst.

**Positives**
- 51.6M SEK net insider buying, 13 buys, zero sells, two distinct buyers — clean, concentrated dip-buying.
- 9.7% FCF yield and 25.3% ROE — genuinely cash-generative for a retailer at P/E 22.
- Dividend raised despite weak quarter, signaling management/board confidence in cash flow.
- Revenue still +6.5% YoY, 3y CAGR +7.3% — growth slowing but not broken.

**Concerns**
- Net debt / equity of 243% is heavy — leverage amplifies any consumer-demand weakness.
- Top-line momentum is decelerating; multiple M4 news items flag near-zero result and weak earnings.
- The insider buying is all **one entity** (Aforber) — it's a major shareholder averaging down, not a broad insider cluster; less diversified signal than it first appears.
- Price still -23.8% from 52w-high and falling through 200ma; no technical bottom confirmed, vol elevated at 49%.
- P/E 22 on a slowing 4.4% net-margin retailer isn't cheap on earnings — the cheapness is in FCF, not P/E.

**Insider / news read.** Strongest part of the case: a major holder bought 51.6M SEK across the dip, corroborated by DI/EFN coverage as a confidence signal. News is mixed — earnings read both as "stable, dividend raised" and "historically weak result," so the market punished it while the insider stepped in.

**Price read.** -18.5% in a month and -23.8% off the high, sitting just below the 200ma. Cost basis 75.50 vs px 79.10 — modestly green. This is a washed-out, high-vol level where the insider is buying; risk/reward favors holding, marginal add only.

**Verdict.** Insider conviction and FCF support the held position, but single-buyer signal + heavy leverage + slowing growth cap upside — HELD: thesis intact.

  "would_sell_at": 95.0,

}
```

--- Analyst note on TEL2-B.ST (Tele2 B) ---
# Tele2 B (TEL2-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Tele2 is a high-quality, cash-generative Nordic telecom with a 6% dividend and 10% FCF yield at P/E 12 — but the Scout angle is **misread**. The 5.4B SEK insider event is a *SELL* by Freya Investissement (a board-linked major holder), not a buy. The bullish signal here is a small CEO buy (~2.3M SEK), not a billion-krona conviction purchase. This is a steady-yield blue-chip, not an aggressive compounder.

**Positives**
- Excellent margins (40% operating, 34% net) and ROE 35% — genuinely high-quality telecom economics.
- 10.3% FCF yield + 6% dividend = real cash return; cheap at P/E 12.
- New CEO Högberg bought ~2.3M SEK personally — modest insider conviction at the top.
- 12m +23% with positive vs-200ma trend; not broken.

**Concerns**
- **Scout angle is wrong**: the 5.4B SEK is a board-member *SELL*, and 90d net is a 5.4B SEK NET SELL. That's the opposite of the surfaced signal.
- Major-shareholder "mega-deal" news is vague — ownership-shift uncertainty is an overhang, not a catalyst I can underwrite.
- Low growth (rev +1.4% YoY, 3y CAGR 2.1%) — this is a yield/value name, not a +50%/6mo vehicle.
- Net debt 24.7B (85% net debt/equity) — fine for telecom but caps re-rating upside.

**Insider / news read.** Net insider flow is a large SELL driven by Freya Investissement; the buys are small management purchases. The "mega-deal" headlines around the major shareholder explain the Freya activity and inject ownership uncertainty — directionally neutral-to-negative until clarified.

**Price read.** 174.75, down 2.5%/1m and 6%/3m, 11.6% off the 52w high but +3% over 200ma. Consolidating, not breaking — no momentum trigger right now.

**Verdict.** Quality income name but no aggressive edge, and the bullish premise is factually inverted. PASS — no edge.

```json

  "would_buy_at": 155.0,

  "notes_for_trader": "Scout mislabeled a 5.4B SEK Freya SELL as a buy — do not size on that signal. Slow-growth yield name, wrong fit for +50%/6mo mandate. Only real insider buy is a small ~2.3M SEK CEO purchase. Pass unless it cheapens materially toward ~155 on a dividend-floor basis."
}
```

--- Analyst note on NOLA-B.ST (Nolato) ---
# Nolato (NOLA-B.ST) — Research Note

**Thesis.** A long-term major shareholder (Herenco Holding) has bought ~SEK 25.3M across three tranches in a single week, near 52-week lows (-24% off high). The setup is a value-with-insider-confidence play: a stable, dividend-paying medical/polymer materials business trading at a depressed multiple after a soft 12 months, with a credible long-term holder leaning in. Upside is mean-reversion, not a sharp catalyst — there's no near-term event identified.

**Positives.**
- Concentrated insider cluster (3 Herenco buys in one week + a board member), zero sells over 90d — a genuine signal on a Swedish name.
- Solid quality: 13% ROE, 10.8% operating margin, positive FCF, low leverage (net debt/equity 19.7%).
- 3.5% dividend yield provides a floor while waiting.

**Concerns.**
- Fundamentals are *deteriorating*, not improving: revenue -3.2% YoY, -4.2% 3y CAGR. Herenco may be buying value, but the business is shrinking.
- P/E 17.2 on a no-growth materials name isn't cheap; FCF yield only 3.4%.
- No near-term catalyst — earnings ~40 days out. This is a slow thesis, not the kind of high-conviction edge that earns a big size in this book.
- Herenco is a strategic long-term holder; their buys reflect confidence but also limited float-moving urgency.

**Insider / news read.** The news flow is entirely the insider-buying story — Herenco adding ~SEK 25M over a week. This is the strongest part of the case: a meaningful, repeated, single-direction insider cluster on a Swedish small/mid cap, exactly the signal §3 weights highly.

**Price read.** At 48.45, down 13.9% over 12m, -12.3% vs 200ma, -24% off the 52w high — washed out and basing (3m +2.4% vs 1m -5.0%). Insider buys are landing right into this weakness, which is constructive.

**Verdict.** The insider cluster is real and worth respecting, but the underlying business is contracting with no near-term catalyst — a starter, not a high-conviction swing. **BUY — starter.**

```json

  "key_concern": "Revenue is shrinking (-3.2% YoY, -4.2% 3y CAGR) on a non-cheap 17x P/E with no near-term catalyst.",
  "would_buy_at": 49.0,
  "would_sell_at": 58.0,
  "notes_for_trader": "Insider-driven value/mean-reversion, slow horizon. Earnings ~Aug 5. Starter size only — thesis is the cluster, not growth. Exit if Herenco buying stops and price breaks below ~45."
}
```


SCOUT SELL / ROTATION FLAGS (held names):
  - ATRLJ-B.ST: weakest holding, -1.9% pnl, -17.3% below 200MA, no insider signal, capital better deployed in HEXA/RUSTA insider clusters
  - CORE-B.ST: down -7.1% pnl, down -34.8% from 200MA, illiquid, real estate exposure — dead weight in concentrated book
  - HM-B.ST: 3.9% of portfolio, flat +2.4% pnl, -3.3% below 200MA — lowest conviction holding, capital needed for insider-backed moves

RISK CAPS (one book — enforced in code):
  - Max 30% in any single holding (of total)
  - Max 40% in any single sector (of total)
  - Max ~8 holdings total
  - Min ~5% cash (max ~95% equity)
  - Long-only: no leverage, shorting, or derivatives
  - No minimum holding period or holding count
response_text:
**Read** — 54% cash and below floor pace; capital is sitting idle. I'm at the 8-holding cap, so any new buy requires a sell. HEXA's insider cluster is the cleanest new signal but fundamentals are corrupted — starter only.

**Decisions:**
- **Sell CORE-B.ST** — Scout-flagged dead weight: -7.1% pnl, -34.8% below 200MA, illiquid, redundant real estate exposure (ATRLJ covers the leveraged-RE insider theme better). Frees a slot.
- **Buy HEXA-B.ST starter** — unanimous insider cluster (4 buyers incl. CFO) into a 21%-below-200MA washout; Jul 25 catalyst. Corrupted fundamentals + 82% vol cap it at a starter (~6% / 7.5k SEK). Funded by ample cash, not the CORE sale per se.
- **Deploy idle cash into HEXA more than a nibble** — 54% cash while below floor is dead weight; sizing HEXA to a real ~6% starter while respecting the volatility.

Keeping ATRLJ and HM for now — Scout flags noted but both have intact insider theses and near-term prints (ATRLJ Jul 10). No need to dump into the earnings window today.
